title: Smoky-Hot Coconut Tagine
description: A jammy, dense Moroccan dish featuring desiccated coconut and a smoky heat, perfect for lazy Sunday mornings or temple-friendly dinners.
instructions: Season chicken with salt and cook in a skillet until golden brown on all sides, about 8 minutes.
Remove from heat and set aside. In the same skillet, add olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in cumin, smoked paprika, turmeric, and desiccated coconut. Cook for another minute.
Pour in chicken broth and bring to a boil. Add cooked chicken back into the pot with any juices remaining from cooking.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until chicken is tender, about 15 minutes.
Stir in coconut milk and lemon juice. Simmer for an additional 5-7 minutes or until mixture thickens slightly.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
